statement: The Earth has four main layers.
explanation: True! The Earth has four layers: the crust, the mantle, the outer core, and the inner core.

statement: The crust is the innermost layer of the Earth.
explanation: False! The crust is the outermost layer. The inner core is at the very centre of the Earth.

statement: The Earth's crust is made of rocks.
explanation: True! The crust is the hard, rocky outer layer that we live on.

statement: The core of the Earth is made of gold and silver.
explanation: False! The core is made of the metals iron and nickel, not gold and silver.

statement: The melted rock inside the mantle is called magma.
explanation: True! The rocks in the mantle are so hot that they melt. This melted rock is called magma.

statement: The outer core is solid because it is under so much pressure.
explanation: False! The outer core is liquid. It is the inner core that is solid due to the extreme force of gravity.

statement: The inner core is solid even though it is extremely hot.
explanation: True! The force of gravity is so strong at the centre that it keeps the inner core in a solid state, even at 6,000 °C.

statement: The inner core is about as hot as the surface of the Sun.
explanation: True! The inner core reaches about 6,000 °C, which is roughly the same temperature as the surface of the Sun.

statement: The mantle is thinner than the crust.
explanation: False! The mantle is much thicker than the crust. The crust is actually the thinnest layer of the Earth.

statement: As you go deeper into the Earth, it generally gets colder.
explanation: False! It actually gets hotter as you go deeper. The inner core is the hottest layer of the Earth.

statement: When magma comes out of a volcano, it is called lava.
explanation: True! Magma is the name for melted rock underground. Once it reaches the surface, we call it lava.

statement: A composite volcano is shaped like a flat table.
explanation: False! A composite volcano is cone-shaped, like an upside-down ice cream cone.

statement: Lava can destroy buildings and plants.
explanation: True! Hot lava burns and buries everything in its path, including crops and buildings.

statement: The crater is the opening at the top of a volcano.
explanation: True! The crater is the bowl-shaped opening at the top of a volcano where material comes out.

statement: Volcanic ash and gases are good for people to breathe.
explanation: False! People become ill when they breathe in volcanic ash and poisonous gases.

statement: Lava and ash can make the soil better for growing crops.
explanation: True! Even though eruptions are destructive, lava creates good soil and ash works as a natural fertilizer.

statement: A volcanic plateau is a deep hole caused by a volcanic eruption.
explanation: False! A volcanic plateau is a flat area of land formed when lava cools and hardens into rock.

statement: A magma chamber is a large pool of liquid rock beneath a volcano.
explanation: True! The magma chamber stores molten rock beneath the surface. When pressure builds, the magma rises and the volcano erupts.

statement: Volcanoes can only cause harm — they have no positive effects.
explanation: False! While volcanoes are destructive, they also create fertile soil, new landforms, and provide geothermal energy.

statement: Geothermal energy comes from heat stored underground in volcanic regions.
explanation: True! Underground water in volcanic areas is heated by magma. This heat can be used to generate electricity.

statement: A vent is a pool of water inside a volcano.
explanation: False! A vent is an opening in a volcano where lava, ash, and gases escape during an eruption.

statement: Earthquakes are caused by movements of rocks in the Earth's crust.
explanation: True! When large rock sections (tectonic plates) in the crust move and rub against each other, they cause earthquakes.

statement: A tsunami is a type of strong wind.
explanation: False! A tsunami is a giant ocean wave, usually caused by an earthquake under the sea.

statement: The epicentre is the point on the Earth's surface directly above where an earthquake starts.
explanation: True! The epicentre is on the surface directly above the underground focus point. It usually has the most damage.

statement: When tectonic plates rub together, the energy transfers into waves.
explanation: True! The friction between the plates creates energy that transfers into seismic waves, causing the ground to shake.

statement: Earthquakes can cause landslides where rocks and soil slide down slopes.
explanation: True! The shaking from earthquakes is strong enough to make rocks and soil slide down slopes, creating landslides.

statement: Tsunami waves get smaller as they approach the coast.
explanation: False! Tsunami waves get bigger as they approach the coast because the water becomes shallower.

statement: The focus and the epicentre of an earthquake are the same thing.
explanation: False! The focus is underground where the earthquake starts. The epicentre is on the surface directly above the focus.

statement: The Pacific Ring of Fire is an area with very few earthquakes and volcanoes.
explanation: False! The Pacific Ring of Fire is an area around the Pacific Ocean where many tectonic plates meet, causing frequent volcanoes and earthquakes.

statement: A seismometer is a tool that measures the strength of earthquakes.
explanation: True! Scientists use seismometers to detect and measure the strength of seismic waves from earthquakes.

statement: A giraffe has a long neck to eat leaves from tall trees.
explanation: True! The giraffe's long neck is an adaptation that helps it reach leaves high up in trees and also see predators from far away.

statement: Fish breathe underwater using their lungs.
explanation: False! Fish use organs called gills to breathe underwater, not lungs.

statement: A duck has webbed feet to help it swim.
explanation: True! The skin between a duck's toes acts like a paddle, helping it push through water efficiently.

statement: Adaptations are special features that help animals survive.
explanation: True! Adaptations are physical features or behaviours that help living things survive in their specific habitat.

statement: A duck has webbed feet so it can grip onto tree branches.
explanation: False! A duck's webbed feet are for swimming, not gripping. Birds that perch on trees have separate, curved toes.

statement: A flamingo's flat beak helps it strain (filter) small plants and animals from water.
explanation: True! The flamingo's beak works like a strainer, filtering tiny organisms from shallow water.

statement: A kingfisher's sharp, pointed beak is used for filtering water.
explanation: False! A kingfisher's sharp beak is used like a spear to catch fish, not to filter water. That's what a flamingo's beak does.

statement: Camouflage helps animals hide from predators by blending in with their surroundings.
explanation: True! Animals with camouflage have colours or patterns that match their environment, making them hard to spot.

statement: An elephant's trunk is an adaptation only used for breathing.
explanation: False! An elephant's trunk is a multi-purpose adaptation used for breathing, drinking water, and grabbing food from trees.

statement: All birds have the same type of beak because they all eat the same food.
explanation: False! Different birds have different beak shapes because they eat different types of food. A flamingo filters water; a kingfisher spears fish.

statement: An orangutan's long arms are an adaptation for living in tropical forests.
explanation: True! Orangutans use their long arms and strong fingers to grip branches and swing through the trees of tropical forests.

statement: The inner core and outer core are both made of the same metals.
explanation: True! Both the inner and outer core are made of iron and nickel. The difference is their state: the inner core is solid and the outer core is liquid.

statement: Magma and lava are the same thing.
explanation: False! Magma is melted rock underground. Once it reaches the surface through a volcano, we call it lava.

statement: Earthquakes can only happen on land, not under the sea.
explanation: False! Earthquakes can happen under the sea too. When they do, they can cause giant waves called tsunamis.

statement: A habitat is the natural home of an animal.
explanation: True! A habitat is the natural environment where an animal lives, finds food, water, and shelter.

statement: We can easily dig down to the Earth's core.
explanation: False! The Earth's layers are thousands of kilometres deep. No one has ever dug past even a small fraction of the crust.

statement: Composite volcanoes are built from alternating layers of ash and lava that have hardened over time.
explanation: True! Each eruption adds new layers of ash and lava. Over many eruptions, these layers build up to form the cone shape.

statement: Bears use their sharp claws mainly for climbing trees.
explanation: False! While some bears can climb, their sharp claws are primarily adapted for catching fish and digging for food.

statement: Tectonic plates are big pieces of the Earth's crust that can move.
explanation: True! The Earth's crust is made of large sections called tectonic plates that fit together like puzzle pieces and slowly move.

statement: An animal's characteristics are the special features that help describe it.
explanation: True! Characteristics are the special features or qualities of a living thing, such as a giraffe's long neck or a duck's webbed feet.

statement: Muscles are joined to the skeleton.
explanation: True! Muscles are attached to bones in the skeleton and pull on them to create movement.

statement: Muscles push on bones to make them move.
explanation: False! Muscles can only pull on bones — they cannot push. Contraction (shortening) pulls the bone.

statement: Muscles work in pairs to move bones.
explanation: True! Muscles always work in pairs with opposite actions — one contracts while the other relaxes.

statement: When one muscle contracts and gets shorter, the other muscle in the pair also contracts.
explanation: False! When one muscle contracts, the other relaxes and gets longer. They work with opposite actions.

statement: Muscles can both push and pull on bones to create movement.
explanation: False! Muscles can only pull (contract). This is why they must work in pairs — one to pull a bone one way, and another to pull it back.

statement: One function of the skeleton is to protect organs.
explanation: True! The skeleton protects vital organs. For example, the skull protects the brain and the rib cage protects the heart and lungs.

statement: The skeleton only helps us move — it has no other job.
explanation: False! The skeleton does three things: it helps us move, gives shape to our body, and protects our organs.

statement: Vertebrates are animals with a backbone.
explanation: True! Vertebrates include fish, birds, reptiles, amphibians, and mammals — all have a backbone.

statement: A worm is a vertebrate.
explanation: False! A worm has no backbone, making it an invertebrate.

statement: A ladybird (ladybug) is an invertebrate.
explanation: True! A ladybird is an insect. All insects are invertebrates — they have no backbone but have an exoskeleton.

statement: An exoskeleton is a skeleton on the outside of an animal's body.
explanation: True! An exoskeleton is a hard outer covering found on invertebrates like insects, crabs, and spiders.

statement: Regular exercise makes our bones and muscles weaker.
explanation: False! Regular exercise makes our bones and muscles stronger and keeps our heart healthy.
